main_contract: LLM mines sparse long-tail event hypotheses and loss hypotheses; training consumes them as dataset-aware losses. LLM output is not used as a verifier, calibrated prior, oracle, or inference-time prediction gate.

experiments.notes: Pure DLinear evaluated on the earlier transferable zero+peak GPT mask.
Raw GPT loss weights with the zero+peak mask fail the overall guardrail and worsen combined event.
Low-LR baseline finetune keeps overall inside 3 percent but zero hypotheses dominate combined event degradation.
Pure DLinear evaluated only on the GPT-5.5 peak long-tail hypothesis.
Loss-only baseline finetune improves peak event MSE by 49.5 percent while staying inside the 3 percent overall guardrail.

conclusion: The corrected GPT-5.5 prompt and normalizer keep LLM output in the intended role: sparse long-tail loss hypotheses, not rule validation or deterministic priors.
Zero-event timestamp hypotheses are not transfer-positive on ETTm1; even without zero_consistency, they worsen combined event MSE.

The GPT-5.5 peak long-tail hypothesis is transfer-positive under loss-only low-learning-rate finetuning: event/peak MSE improves from 0.051324 to 0.025899 while overall MSE remains within the 3 percent guardrail.
The current main ETTm1 rule file keeps one-off zero evidence as explicit train windows so it will not create false-positive test zero events.
